chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Divorce by an Insane Person

text: 4770 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلْكَرِيمِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ طَلاَقِ اَلْمَعْتُوهِ اَلزَّائِلِ اَلْعَقْلِ أَ يَجُوزُ فَقَالَ "لاَ" وَ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا كَانَتْ كَذَلِكَ يَجُوزُ بَيْعُهَا وَ صَدَقَتُهَا فَقَالَ "لاَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4770 - Abd al-Karim ibn ʿAmr narrated from al-Ḥalabi from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about the divorce of a mentally impaired person whose intellect is diminished, whether it is permissible?
Imam (as) replied: "No."
I (narrator) also asked about a woman in the same condition, whether it is permissible to sell her property or manage her dowry?
He replied: "No."
